# anyscale_policy_binding (Resource)

**BETA FEATURE**: Manages policy bindings for SCIM provisioning. Policy bindings define which user groups have which roles on a resource (organization, cloud, or project).

**IMPORTANT**: Policy bindings REPLACE all existing group-based permissions on the resource. Any role bindings not specified will be removed. Setting an empty `bindings` list removes all group permissions.

**Role Validation**:
- **Organization**: `owner`, `collaborator`
- **Cloud**: `collaborator`, `readonly`
- **Project**: `owner`, `write`, `readonly`

**Access Requirements**:
- Groups must have cloud access before granting project access in that cloud
- If a group has `readonly` on a cloud, they can only have `readonly` on projects in that cloud
- If a group has `collaborator` on a cloud, they can have any role on projects
- Organization owners cannot be added to cloud/project policies (they have implicit access)

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Basic cloud policy binding with readonly access
resource "anyscale_policy_binding" "cloud_readonly" {
  resource_type = "cloud"
  resource_id   = "cld_abc123"

  bindings = [
    {
      role_name = "readonly"
      principals = [
        "ug_team1",
        "ug_team2"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

# Cloud policy with multiple roles
resource "anyscale_policy_binding" "cloud_multi_role" {
  resource_type = "cloud"
  resource_id   = "cld_xyz789"

  bindings = [
    {
      role_name = "collaborator"
      principals = [
        "ug_engineering"
      ]
    },
    {
      role_name = "readonly"
      principals = [
        "ug_analysts",
        "ug_viewers"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

# Project policy binding
resource "anyscale_policy_binding" "project_permissions" {
  resource_type = "project"
  resource_id   = "prj_def456"

  bindings = [
    {
      role_name = "owner"
      principals = [
        "ug_project_owners"
      ]
    },
    {
      role_name = "write"
      principals = [
        "ug_developers"
      ]
    },
    {
      role_name = "readonly"
      principals = [
        "ug_stakeholders"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

# Organization policy binding
resource "anyscale_policy_binding" "organization" {
  resource_type = "organization"
  resource_id   = "org_ghi789"

  bindings = [
    {
      role_name = "owner"
      principals = [
        "ug_admins"
      ]
    },
    {
      role_name = "collaborator"
      principals = [
        "ug_all_users"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

# Empty bindings - removes all group-based permissions
resource "anyscale_policy_binding" "no_group_access" {
  resource_type = "cloud"
  resource_id   = "cld_private"
  bindings      = []
}

# Using data sources to reference user groups
data "anyscale_user_group" "engineering" {
  name = "Engineering"
}

data "anyscale_cloud" "production" {
  name = "production-cloud"
}

resource "anyscale_policy_binding" "prod_cloud_access" {
  resource_type = "cloud"
  resource_id   = data.anyscale_cloud.production.id

  bindings = [
    {
      role_name  = "collaborator"
      principals = [data.anyscale_user_group.engineering.id]
    }
  ]
}

# Output the sync status
output "policy_sync_status" {
  value       = anyscale_policy_binding.cloud_readonly.sync_status
  description = "Status of policy synchronization"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bindings` (Attributes List) List of role bindings for this resource. Empty list removes all group permissions. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--bindings))
- `resource_id` (String) The ID of the resource (e.g., `org_abc123`, `cld_xyz789`, `prj_def456`).
- `resource_type` (String) The type of resource. Must be `organization`, `cloud`, or `project`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier for this policy binding (format: `resource_type/resource_id`).
- `sync_status` (String) The synchronization status of the policy (e.g., `success`, `pending`, `failed`).

<a id="nestedatt--bindings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `bindings`

Required:

- `principals` (List of String) List of user group IDs (format: `ug_*`) assigned to this role.
- `role_name` (String) The name of the role. Valid values depend on resource type:
  - **Organization**: `owner`, `collaborator`
  - **Cloud**: `collaborator`, `readonly`
  - **Project**: `owner`, `write`, `readonly`
